origin.name: Albania
origin.death_registration: Zyra e Gjendjes Civile (Civil Status Office)
origin.key_document: Vertetika e vdekjes (death certificate from Civil Status Office)
origin.source: FCDO Albania travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Tirana guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Albania is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Vertetika e vdekjes (death certificate, certified English translation required)
special_rules: Albania is a Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered at the Zyra e Gjendjes Civile (Civil Status Office) promptly.
All Albanian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Albanian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Tirana can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Albania to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Albania. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil Status Office, apostille, and documentation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
